chief_complaint: A 72-year-old male presented with chronic stroke symptoms, specifically hemiparesis of the upper arm.
history: The patient underwent a 20-day virtual reality exergaming program using a commercially available bundle of supplies, including a head-mounted display and adaptive software. The program aimed to improve hemiparetic arm function.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided. However, the patient's upper-arm function was assessed using the Wolf Motor Function Test (WMFT) and Disabilities of the Arm, Shoulder, and Hand Questionnaire (DASH).
labs: No laboratory tests were mentioned in the case report.
